docs.title: Wellness Platform - Product Documentation
docs.text: Enterprise PRD suite for a hybrid DTC + curated marketplace wellness brand platform, India-first.

docs.title: Document Suite
docs.text:
This documentation covers the full product specification for building a wellness e-commerce platform that combines three surfaces:

  1. Branded DTC Storefront -- own supplements & skincare products sold directly to consumers
  2. Curated Marketplace -- vetted third-party wellness sellers (invite-only)
  3. Editorial Content Hub -- wellness articles, expert advice, ingredient deep-dives

docs.title: Documents
docs.text: # Document Purpose Audience 1 BMRD Business case, market analysis, personas, regulatory landscape, unit economics Stakeholders, investors 2 PRD Feature modules with user stories, acceptance criteria, edge cases Design, Engineering, AI agents 3 IA Sitemap, navigation, URL architecture, content taxonomy, search/filter specs Design, Engineering 4 TAD Platform evaluation (Shopify analysis), tech stack, integrations, data architecture Engineering, DevOps 5 FSD State machines, business rules, complex flows, API contracts Engineering, QA, AI agents

docs.title: Key Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Value Business Model Hybrid (own brand + curated third-party sellers) Product Categories Supplements & Nutrition, Skincare & Beauty Community Editorial content hub (blog, expert advice) Purchases One-time only (no subscriptions) Geography India first Seller Model Curated / invite-only Platform Recommendation Custom headless (Next.js + Medusa.js + Strapi) -- Shopify not recommended

docs.title: Phasing
docs.text:
• Phase 1 (MVP): Own-brand storefront + content hub + full shopping flow
  • Phase 2: Marketplace layer (seller onboarding, multi-seller checkout, payouts)
  • Phase 3: Personalization, loyalty, native mobile app, AI recommendations

docs.title: Document Conventions
docs.text:
These documents are structured for dual consumption by human developers and AI coding agents:

  • Every feature has a unique ID (e.g., F-CAT-001, F-MKT-003)
  • Every business rule has a unique ID (e.g., BR-CHK-001)
  • Acceptance criteria are testable checklists
  • State machines define all valid state transitions
  • RFC 2119 language: MUST / SHOULD / MAY
  • All data types and constraints are explicit

docs.title: BMRD: Business & Market Requirements Document
docs.text: Document ID: BMRD-v1.0 Status: Draft Author: [Product Lead / Founder] Last Updated: [Date] Audience: Stakeholders, investors, executive team, product team

docs.title: 1. Executive Summary
docs.text:
This document establishes the business case for building a hybrid wellness e-commerce platform that combines three surfaces:

  1. Branded DTC Storefront -- selling the company's own supplements, nutrition, and skincare products directly to consumers
  2. Curated Marketplace -- hosting vetted third-party wellness sellers (invite-only) who list their products alongside the brand's own catalog
  3. Editorial Content Hub -- publishing wellness articles, expert advice, and ingredient deep-dives that drive organic traffic and build brand authority

The platform launches India-first, targeting health-conscious urban consumers in Tier 1 and Tier 2 cities. Revenue comes from three streams: own-product margins, marketplace commissions on third-party sales, and featured placement fees from sellers.

The hybrid model is the core differentiator. Unlike Nykaa (pure marketplace) or Healthkart (single-brand DTC), this platform offers the trust of a curated brand experience with the catalog breadth of a marketplace -- similar to how Hims built brand authority before expanding, but adapted for the Indian market with multi-seller support from day one of Phase 2.

docs.title: 2.1 North Star Metric
docs.text: Monthly Repeat Purchase Rate (MRPR): Percentage of customers who make a second purchase within 60 days. This metric captures product quality, trust, and platform stickiness better than GMV alone.

docs.title: 2.2 Success Metrics
docs.text: Metric Target (Month 6) Target (Month 12) Measurement Method GMV (Gross Merchandise Value) ₹25L/month ₹1Cr/month Order data Monthly Active Users (MAU) 50,000 200,000 Analytics (GA4/Mixpanel) Monthly Repeat Purchase Rate 15% 25% Cohort analysis Average Order Value (AOV) ₹800 ₹1,200 Order data Own Brand vs Marketplace GMV Split 100% own (Phase 1) 60:40 own:marketplace Order data Approved Sellers (marketplace) 0 (Phase 1) 30-50 Seller admin panel Content Hub Monthly Visits 20,000 100,000 Analytics Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) ₹300 ₹200 (declining) Marketing spend / new customers Customer Lifetime Value (LTV) ₹2,000 ₹4,000 Revenue per customer over 12 months LTV:CAC Ratio >3:1 >5:1 Derived NPS (Net Promoter Score) >40 >50 Post-purchase surveys

docs.title: 2.3 Business Objectives
docs.text:
1. Establish brand authority in the Indian wellness space through curated products and expert content
  2. Build a defensible marketplace moat by onboarding high-quality sellers that consumers cannot find on Amazon/Flipkart
  3. Achieve unit economics viability within 12 months (positive contribution margin on own-brand, breakeven on marketplace operations)
  4. Create a content-driven acquisition channel that reduces dependency on paid advertising over time

docs.title: 3.1 Market Sizing
docs.text:
India Health & Wellness Market: - Market size: USD 164.35 billion (2025), projected to reach USD 257.94 billion by 2034 - CAGR: 5.14% - Key growth drivers: rising health awareness post-COVID, increasing disposable income in Tier 2/3 cities, growing digital health literacy

Supplements & Nutrition (India): - Nutraceuticals market: ~USD 10-12 billion (2024) - Key sub-segments: vitamins & minerals, protein supplements, immunity boosters, herbal/Ayurvedic supplements - Growth driven by: fitness culture adoption, preventive healthcare awareness, D2C brand proliferation

Skincare & Beauty (India): - Market size: ~USD 18-20 billion (2024) - Online penetration growing rapidly (estimated 15-20% of total) - Key sub-segments: face care, anti-aging, natural/clean beauty, men's grooming - Growth driven by: social media influence, ingredient-conscious consumers, premiumization trend

Serviceable Addressable Market (SAM): Online wellness products (supplements + skincare) targeting urban India (Tier 1/2 cities, age 22-45, smartphone users) -- estimated at USD 2-3 billion.

Serviceable Obtainable Market (SOM): Year 1 target: ₹12Cr GMV (~USD 1.4M), representing <0.1% of SAM.

docs.title: 3.2 Competitive Landscape
docs.text: Competitor Model Strengths Weaknesses Differentiator vs. Us Nykaa Marketplace + private labels Massive catalog (5,000+ brands), strong brand recognition, IPO-funded scale Cluttered UX, no wellness curation, mass market positioning We are curated + expert-content-driven, not a catalog dump Purplle Marketplace Price-competitive, strong Tier 2/3 penetration, 70% YoY active user growth Low brand trust for wellness/health products, discount-driven We lead with trust (curation, FSSAI verification, expert content) Healthkart DTC + marketplace Strong supplements focus, HealthVit + MuscleBlaze owned brands, established trust No skincare, male-skewing, no content hub We serve both genders, combine supplements + skincare + content WOW Skin Science DTC (single brand) Strong brand, Amazon bestseller, ingredient-led marketing Single brand limits catalog, no marketplace, no community We offer multi-brand curated selection + editorial content Amazon India Open marketplace Unlimited selection, Prime delivery, price competition Zero curation, counterfeit concerns, no wellness expertise We solve the trust problem with curation + FSSAI verification 1mg (Tata Health) Marketplace + telehealth Pharma + supplements, doctor consultations, Tata backing Pharmacy-first UX, not a lifestyle/wellness brand We are lifestyle wellness, not clinical/pharmaceutical

docs.title: 3.3 Differentiation Thesis
docs.text:
The Indian wellness e-commerce market has a "trust gap":

  • Open marketplaces (Amazon, Flipkart) offer everything but consumers cannot verify product authenticity, ingredient sourcing, or regulatory compliance
  • Single-brand DTC (WOW, Mamaearth) solves trust for one brand but limits selection
  • Beauty marketplaces (Nykaa, Purplle) are catalog-first, not wellness-expertise-first

The hybrid model bridges this gap: 1. Own-brand products establish quality baseline and margin control 2. Curated sellers expand catalog without diluting trust (every seller is vetted, every product is reviewed) 3. Expert content (journal) educates consumers and drives organic acquisition 4. FSSAI/regulatory badge system builds trust at the product level

docs.title: Persona 1: "Priya" -- The Wellness-Curious Professional
docs.text: Attribute Detail Demographics Female, 26-34, urban (Bangalore/Mumbai/Delhi), household income ₹10-25L/year Occupation IT professional, startup employee, or freelancer Digital behavior Instagram-heavy, follows wellness influencers, reads ingredient lists, uses PhonePe/GPay for payments Buying behavior Researches 3-5 products before buying, reads reviews, price-sensitive but willing to pay premium for trusted brands, prefers COD for first purchase from new brand Pain points Overwhelmed by choices on Amazon, skeptical of "natural" claims, worried about counterfeit supplements Wellness goals Skin health, immunity, energy, stress management Acquisition channels Instagram ads, Google search ("best vitamin C serum India"), wellness blog content

docs.title: Persona 2: "Rahul" -- The Fitness-Focused Buyer
docs.text: Attribute Detail Demographics Male, 24-35, urban (Tier 1/2 cities), household income ₹8-20L/year Occupation Corporate employee, gym-goer, weekend athlete Digital behavior YouTube fitness content, Reddit for supplement reviews, UPI-primary payments Buying behavior Brand-loyal once trust is established, bulk buyer (monthly protein supply), compares prices across platforms, comfortable with online payment Pain points Quality concerns with unbranded supplements, confusion about dosages and combinations, wants expert guidance not just product listings Wellness goals Muscle building, recovery, general health maintenance Acquisition channels Google search ("whey protein India genuine"), YouTube reviews, fitness community referrals

docs.title: Persona 3: "Meera" -- The Gifting-Oriented Buyer
docs.text: Attribute Detail Demographics Female/Male, 30-45, urban, household income ₹15L+/year Occupation Senior professional, business owner Digital behavior WhatsApp-primary, moderate Instagram, uses credit cards and UPI Buying behavior Buys wellness products as gifts (Diwali, birthdays, corporate gifting), values premium packaging, higher AOV, less price-sensitive Pain points Difficulty finding curated wellness gift sets, wants something that "looks premium" not clinical Wellness goals Gift-giving, personal skincare routine Acquisition channels Google search ("wellness gift box India"), Instagram ads, word of mouth

docs.title: Persona 4: "Kavitha" -- The Curated Seller
docs.text: Attribute Detail Demographics 28-45, runs a D2C wellness brand (small-to-mid scale), based anywhere in India Business profile 1-50 SKUs, has FSSAI/manufacturing licenses, sells on own website + Amazon, wants additional distribution channels with higher margins than Amazon Pain points Amazon takes 15-30% commission + advertising costs, no brand storytelling on marketplace platforms, competition from counterfeits What she wants from us Curated platform with brand story pages, fair commission (10-15%), reliable payouts, co-marketing opportunities Acquisition channels LinkedIn outreach, industry events, seller referrals, inbound via "Sell With Us" page

docs.title: 5.1 Revenue Streams
docs.text: Stream Description Expected Contribution (Year 1) Own-Brand Product Sales Direct margin on products sourced/manufactured under the brand's own label 70% of revenue Marketplace Commission Percentage of GMV from third-party seller transactions 20% of revenue Featured Placement Fees Sellers pay for homepage features, category page top spots, content hub product embeds 10% of revenue

docs.title: 5.2 Commission Structure (Marketplace)
docs.text:
Category Base Commission Fulfillment Fee (if platform-fulfilled) Supplements & Nutrition 12% +₹30-50/order Skincare & Beauty 15% +₹30-50/order Bundles / Gift Sets 10% +₹50-80/order

Rationale: Lower than Amazon India (15-30%) to attract quality sellers. Higher commission on skincare (higher margins in category). Lower on bundles (incentivize AOV growth).

docs.title: 5.3 Own-Brand Unit Economics (per order)
docs.text: Line Item Amount (₹) % of Revenue Average Selling Price (ASP) 800 100% COGS (product cost) 280 35% Packaging & labeling 40 5% Shipping (avg, via Shiprocket) 60 7.5% Payment gateway fee (Razorpay ~2%) 16 2% Returns & RTO (~8% return rate) 64 8% Contribution Margin 340 42.5%

docs.title: 5.4 Marketplace Unit Economics (per order)
docs.text: Line Item Amount (₹) Notes GMV per order (avg) 1,000 Marketplace ASP slightly higher Commission revenue (12-15%) 120-150 Platform's revenue Payment gateway fee on full GMV 20 Platform bears this Payout processing fee 5-10 Razorpay Route / Cashfree Net marketplace revenue per order 90-120 Before fixed costs

docs.title: 5.5 Payment Gateway Comparison
docs.text:
Gateway Transaction Fee UPI Support Marketplace Split (Route/Split) Settlement Cycle Notes Razorpay 2% (cards), 0% (UPI < ₹2000) Native Razorpay Route (split payments, escrow) T+2 (standard) Best marketplace support, most popular in India PayU 1.99% (cards), 0% (UPI) Native PayU Hub (marketplace payouts) T+2 Strong alternative, competitive pricing Cashfree 1.90% (cards), 0% (UPI) Native Cashfree Payouts (bank transfer API) T+1 (available) Fastest settlement, good payout API

Recommendation: Razorpay as primary gateway. Best marketplace split-payment support (Razorpay Route), broadest payment method coverage, strongest developer documentation.

docs.title: 6.1 FSSAI (Food Safety and Standards Authority of India)
docs.text:
Applicable to: All supplements, nutrition products, nutraceuticals, health foods.

Requirement Applicability Detail FSSAI License (Central) Own brand (manufacturer/marketer) Central license required if annual turnover > ₹12Cr or operating in 2+ states. Requires Form B application, product formulation details, manufacturing premises inspection FSSAI License (State) Smaller own-brand operations State license if turnover ₹12L-20Cr, single state Seller FSSAI Verification Marketplace obligation Platform MUST verify that every seller listing supplements/nutrition holds a valid FSSAI license before allowing product listings Product-Level Compliance All supplement listings Each product must display: FSSAI license number, ingredient list, nutritional information, best before date, allergen warnings, "Not for medicinal use" disclaimer (for nutraceuticals) Health Claims Restrictions Content + product descriptions FSSAI regulates health claims on supplements. Cannot claim to "cure" or "treat" diseases. Only structure/function claims allowed (e.g., "supports immunity") Labeling Requirements All products MRP, net quantity, manufacturer address, FSSAI logo, vegetarian/non-vegetarian symbol (mandatory in India), batch number

Categories under FSSAI Nutraceuticals Regulation: 1. Health Supplements 2. Nutraceuticals 3. Food for Special Dietary Use 4. Food for Special Medical Purpose 5. Specialty Food containing Plant/Botanical ingredients 6. Probiotics 7. Prebiotics 8. Novel Foods

docs.title: 6.2 Drugs & Cosmetics Act (Skincare & Beauty)
docs.text: Requirement Detail Cosmetics Registration Products classified as "cosmetics" under D&C Act require registration with CDSCO (Central Drugs Standard Control Organization) Import License If importing skincare products, import registration under D&C Act is required Ingredient Restrictions BIS (Bureau of Indian Standards) IS 4707 lists prohibited and restricted cosmetic ingredients Labeling Must include: ingredients (INCI names), manufacturer, batch number, MRP, manufacturing/expiry dates, net content Claims Restrictions Cannot claim "medicinal" or "drug-like" effects. "Anti-aging" is allowed; "cures acne" is not Seller Verification Marketplace must verify seller holds appropriate cosmetics manufacturing license or import registration

docs.title: 6.3 GST Structure
docs.text:
Product Category HSN Code GST Rate Notes Vitamins & minerals 2106 90 12% Health supplements category Protein supplements (whey, plant-based) 2106 90 18% Classified as food preparations Nutraceutical capsules/tablets 2936 / 2937 18% Depending on formulation Skincare - face creams, serums 3304 28% Beauty/cosmetics rate Skincare - sunscreen 3304 28% Same as cosmetics Herbal/Ayurvedic products 3003 / 3004 12% If registered as Ayurvedic medicine Gift sets (mixed) Highest rate component 18-28% GST on composite supply = highest rate

Multi-Seller GST Invoicing: - For marketplace orders, each seller must issue their own GST invoice with their GSTIN - Platform generates invoice on behalf of seller (facilitation model) or seller issues directly - TCS (Tax Collected at Source): E-commerce operators must collect TCS at 1% (0.5% CGST + 0.5% SGST) on net taxable supplies of sellers and file GSTR-8 - Platform must maintain seller-wise GSTIN records and validate them

docs.title: 6.4 Consumer Protection (E-Commerce) Rules, 2020
docs.text:
As a marketplace entity, the platform MUST:

Obligation Implementation Display seller details Show seller legal name, GSTIN, address, rating on every product page Product information accuracy Ensure sellers provide accurate images, descriptions, ingredients. Platform reviews before publishing Return/refund policy display Clear return policy on every product page, per-seller if policies differ Grievance redressal Acknowledge complaints within 48 hours, resolve within 30 days. Appoint Grievance Officer, display contact on website No preferential treatment disclosure If any seller (including own brand) gets preferential placement, disclose it Price manipulation prohibition No artificial price inflation before sales/discounts Country of origin Display country of origin for all products (mandatory) Seller credential verification Verify seller identity documents, maintain records IP infringement records Maintain records of reported intellectual property violations Ranking criteria transparency Disclose how search results and product rankings are determined

docs.title: 6.5 Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (DPDP)
docs.text: Requirement Implementation Consent management Explicit consent before collecting personal data. Granular consent (marketing vs transactional) Data minimization Collect only data necessary for the transaction/service Purpose limitation Use data only for stated purpose Data principal rights Users can request access, correction, erasure of their data Data breach notification Notify Data Protection Board and affected users in case of breach Cross-border transfer Restrictions on transferring personal data outside India (to be specified by government notification) Children's data Extra protections if platform serves users under 18

docs.title: 6.6 Other Regulatory Considerations
docs.text: Regulation Relevance Legal Metrology Act MRP display, net quantity declarations on all packaged products BIS Standards Certain product categories may require BIS certification FDI Policy Marketplace model allows 100% FDI; inventory-based model has restrictions. Hybrid model must clearly separate platform entity from own-brand entity GST Registration Platform entity needs GST registration in every state where it has business presence Shops & Establishments Act State-specific registrations for business operations

docs.title: 7. Stakeholder Map & RACI
docs.text:
Activity Founder Product Lead Eng Lead Design Lead Legal Operations Business strategy & funding A C I I C I Product requirements (PRD) C A R R C C Technical architecture (TAD) I C A C I I UI/UX design C C C A I I Regulatory compliance C C I I A C Seller onboarding operations C C I I C A Content strategy & creation C A I C C I Shipping & logistics setup I C C I I A Payment gateway integration I C A I C C

Legend: A = Accountable, R = Responsible, C = Consulted, I = Informed

docs.title: 8.1 Constraints
docs.text: Constraint Impact India-only launch No international shipping, no multi-currency, no international payment methods needed at launch One-time purchases only No subscription billing, no recurring payment integration, no auto-refill logic needed Curated/invite-only marketplace Seller self-registration is not open. Seller onboarding volume is manually manageable (target: 5-10 sellers/month) Established brand identity Logo, colors, typography, tone of voice are finalized. No brand discovery or naming exercise needed Product categories fixed at launch Supplements & Nutrition, Skincare & Beauty only. No fitness equipment, apparel, or telehealth

docs.title: 8.2 Assumptions
docs.text: Assumption Risk if Wrong 70%+ of traffic will be mobile If desktop-heavy (unlikely for India), mobile-first design decisions may under-serve desktop UX UPI will be the dominant payment method (50%+) If COD dominates, RTO (return-to-origin) rates will be higher, impacting unit economics Sellers will accept 12-15% commission If sellers demand lower, marketplace unit economics suffer. Mitigate by offering value (brand storytelling, co-marketing) FSSAI verification can be done manually at launch scale If seller volume grows beyond 50, need automated FSSAI license verification API Content hub will meaningfully contribute to organic traffic within 6 months If SEO traction is slow, may need to increase paid acquisition budget

docs.title: 9. Risks & Mitigations
docs.text: Risk Likelihood Impact Mitigation Regulatory risk: FSSAI or cosmetics compliance violation from a marketplace seller Medium High (platform liability, fines, reputational damage) Mandatory document upload + manual verification before seller goes live. Periodic re-verification. Immediate delisting on compliance breach Platform risk: Chosen tech platform cannot scale or has critical limitations Medium High (re-platforming cost) Detailed platform evaluation in TAD. Start with modular architecture that allows component replacement Supply chain risk: Own-brand product supply disruption Medium Medium Maintain 30-day buffer stock. Diversify suppliers. Marketplace sellers provide catalog resilience Competition risk: Nykaa or Amazon launches a curated wellness vertical High Medium Speed to market. Build brand loyalty and content moat before incumbents can replicate curation Trust risk: Consumers don't trust a new wellness marketplace High High Launch own-brand first (Phase 1) to build trust. Add marketplace sellers only after brand is established. FSSAI badge system. Expert content builds credibility Seller acquisition risk: Cannot attract quality sellers Medium Medium Start with direct outreach to known D2C brands. Offer lower commission than Amazon. Provide brand story pages as value-add COD/RTO risk: High cash-on-delivery rates lead to return-to-origin losses Medium Medium OTP verification for COD orders above ₹1,000. Incentivize prepaid with ₹50 discount. Track RTO by pincode, block high-RTO areas for COD Data privacy risk: DPDP Act non-compliance Low High Implement consent management from day one. Privacy policy review by legal counsel. Data minimization by design

docs.title: Appendix A: Glossary
docs.text: Term Definition GMV Gross Merchandise Value -- total value of goods sold through the platform AOV Average Order Value -- average revenue per transaction CAC Customer Acquisition Cost -- cost to acquire one new customer LTV Lifetime Value -- total revenue from a customer over their relationship with the platform MRPR Monthly Repeat Purchase Rate -- % of customers making a second purchase within 60 days RTO Return to Origin -- when a shipped order is returned to the seller (common with COD) TCS Tax Collected at Source -- 1% tax e-commerce operators must collect on seller transactions FSSAI Food Safety and Standards Authority of India CDSCO Central Drugs Standard Control Organization BIS Bureau of Indian Standards DPDP Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 HSN Harmonized System of Nomenclature -- classification code for GST GSTIN Goods and Services Tax Identification Number UPI Unified Payments Interface COD Cash on Delivery

docs.text:

docs.title: PRD: Product Requirements Document
docs.text: Document ID: PRD-v1.0 Status: Draft Author: [Product Manager] Last Updated: [Date] Audience: Engineering, Design, QA, AI coding agents Dependencies: BMRD (Doc 01)

docs.title: 1.1 Vision Statement
docs.text: Build the most trusted wellness e-commerce platform in India by combining curated product selection, transparent quality verification, and expert-driven content -- enabling health-conscious consumers to buy wellness products with confidence, not confusion.

docs.title: 1.2 Scope Boundary
docs.text: IN SCOPE (MVP - Phase 1) OUT OF SCOPE Own-brand product catalog (supplements + skincare) Marketplace (Phase 2) Product listing, detail, search, category navigation Subscription/auto-replenish Cart, checkout (single-seller) International shipping UPI, Cards, Net Banking, Wallets, COD Telehealth / virtual consultations Content hub (blog/journal) with product linking Personalization quiz (a la Meology) User accounts (OTP login, profile, orders, wishlist) Loyalty / rewards program Admin panel (catalog, orders, content, analytics) Mobile native app (responsive web only) Responsive mobile-first web design AI-powered recommendations GST invoice generation (own brand) Social features / forums / user-generated content Pincode-based delivery estimation Gift cards / store credit Basic coupon/promo system Multi-language support IN SCOPE (Phase 2) IN SCOPE (Phase 3) Seller application + onboarding Personalization / quiz Multi-seller cart + split checkout Loyalty & rewards program Seller dashboard Native mobile app Commission + payout engine AI recommendations Product moderation workflow Multi-language (Hindi, regional) Multi-seller GST invoicing Gift cards / store credit Seller performance metrics Advanced analytics / BI
